name: Khafiz
bio:
Physical Appearance: Redguard male. Wears a dark green shirt, tattered pants, leather gauntlets, and boots - practical, worn clothing befitting a pirate fence. Armed with a steel cutlass and carries an orange.
Race: Redguard
Personality Keywords: professional, accommodating, merchant, practical
Role: Khafiz is a fence who operates from Dunbarrow Cove after being hired for 1,000 gold through Dahlia Rackham. He buys stolen goods and sells them to the Thieves Guild, providing a convenient outlet for thieves. He also sells lockpicks, weapons, armor (including enchanted items), and offers training in Mercantile up to skill level 75.
Location: Khafiz operates from two locations at Dunbarrow Cove: a dry cave in the lower level where he conducts most of his business, and the deck of the Black Flag where he takes meals.
Daily Routine: From 10am to 11pm, Khafiz can be found in his cave in the lower level of Dunbarrow Cove, buying stolen goods and conducting business. At 11pm, he heads to the deck of the Black Flag for dinner, remaining there until 2am. He then returns to his cave to sleep. If his cave bedroll is occupied by Tahm Blackwell, he either stands around or eventually makes his way to the bedroll near the bonfire in the northeastern corner of the cove, where he sleeps until 10am.
Business Services: Khafiz buys any items, including stolen goods, providing a private fence right at Dunbarrow Cove. Items sold to him count toward Independent Thievery progress, making him particularly valuable for advancing as a thief. He sells lockpicks, weapons, and armor, including enchanted items. He offers Mercantile training up to level 75, and those with Expert Mercantile skill can invest in him to increase his available gold.
Equipment: Khafiz carries a steel cutlass for protection and keeps an orange with him - perhaps for sustenance during his long hours in the cave.
Unusual Training Availability: Khafiz will provide Mercantile training even while asleep, though he won't be available for bartering during those hours. This suggests either exceptional dedication to his teaching role or simply a convenient arrangement.
Personality - The Accommodating Merchant: Khafiz provides convenient and professional fencing services, establishing himself in a cave location that offers privacy and easy access for those living at Dunbarrow Cove. His willingness to train in Mercantile even when his own skill level is lower than what he teaches suggests either exceptional teaching ability or a practical approach to supplementing his income. He maintains regular business hours from 10am to 11pm, showing reliability and structure in his operations. When his sleeping arrangements are disrupted by Tahm Blackwell occupying his bedroll, he adapts by finding alternative sleeping spots rather than creating conflict, demonstrating a flexible and practical nature.
author: Bethesda
